audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Timothy text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for later departures, false asceticism, creation goodness, training in reverence, youth, public reading/teaching, gift.

verses.restored: But the Spirit expressly says that in later seasons some will depart from the trust, paying attention to misleading spirits and teachings of daimons,
in hypocrisy of false-speakers, having been seared in their own conscience,
forbidding to marry, commanding abstinence from foods that the Divine created to be received with thanksgiving by those trusting and knowing the truth.
Because every creature/created thing of the Divine is beautiful/good, and nothing is to be rejected when received with thanksgiving,
for it is sanctified through word of Divine and prayer.
Putting these things before the siblings, you will be a beautiful/good servant of Messiah Jesus, being nourished in the words of the trust and of the beautiful/good teaching that you have followed.
But profane and old-womanish myths refuse; train yourself toward reverence/godliness.
For bodily training is beneficial for a little, but reverence/godliness is beneficial for all things, having promise of life now and of the coming one.
Trustworthy is the saying and worthy of full welcome.
For into this we labor and struggle, because we have hoped upon living Divine, who is Savior/Healer of all humans, especially of trusting ones.
Let no one despise your youth, but become a pattern for the trusting ones in word, in conduct, in love, in trust, in purity.
Until I come, pay attention to the public reading, to the encouragement/appeal, to the teaching.
Do not neglect the grace-gift in you, which was given to you through prophecy with laying on of the hands of the council of elders.
Practice these things; be in them, so that your progress may be manifest to all.
Pay attention to yourself and to the teaching; remain in them. For doing this, you will save/heal both yourself and those hearing you.

translation_notes: The anti-ascetic section is body/creation-positive: created things are good and received with thanksgiving.
Bodily training is not rejected; it is relativized beside reverence/godliness.
Daimons is transliterated in notes as hostile spiritual teachings without overdeveloping later demonology.
